title: Mauritius becomes 70th nation to sign the Artemis Accords
summary: Mauritius signed the Artemis Accords on July 17, becoming the 70th nation to join the international agreement in under six years.

title: Space Force triples launch contract ceiling amid rising demand
summary: The U.S. Space Force increased the maximum value of its National Security Space Launch Phase 3 Lane 1 contract from $5.6 billion to $17 billion to meet growing demand. This move reflects the rising need for secure and reliable space launch services.

title: Pentagon awards $7.1 million to expand U.S. production of cover glass for satellites
summary: The Pentagon awarded $7.1 million to Martin Materials Solutions to expand U.S. production of radiation-resistant cover glass for satellites. The funding, provided under the Defense Production Act, supports spacecraft solar arrays and thermal-control systems.

title: Skyroot Aerospace reaches orbit on first Vikram-1 launch
summary: Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket successfully reached orbit on its first launch on July 18, marking the first commercial Indian rocket to achieve this milestone.

title: NASA terminates Draper lunar lander mission
summary: NASA and Draper have canceled their lunar lander mission due to major delays in spacecraft development. The termination marks a setback for planned lunar exploration efforts.

title: SpaceX’s only problem is finding more space to work with
summary: SpaceX's IPO has made the space industry a mainstream capital markets story, highlighting its vision for interplanetary civilization. The company's primary challenge now is securing sufficient space for its expanding operations.

title: Serbia signs the Artemis Accords
summary: Serbia has joined the U.S.-led Artemis Accords, aligning with international lunar exploration efforts. This follows Serbia's earlier participation in China's lunar base project.

title: SpaceX aborts Starship Flight 13 launch attempt
summary: SpaceX aborted the launch of its Starship Flight 13 on July 16 due to some engines failing to ignite. This marks another delay in the vehicle's testing and development process.

title: SpaceX launches 21 satellites for military data network
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 21 satellites for the Space Development Agency's military data network, completing half of the Tranche 1 Transport Layer constellation. The satellites, built by York Space Systems, mark a significant milestone in the agency's planned orbital infrastructure.

title: Nature study identifies a new way to spot nuclear weapons in orbit
summary: A study in Nature Astronomy proposes a new satellite design to detect thermonuclear warheads in orbit. The research is partially funded by unspecified sources.

title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers how black holes feed themselves
summary: Astronomers have used the James Webb Space Telescope to observe how supermassive black holes feed themselves. The discovery provides new insights into the feeding mechanisms of these cosmic phenomena.

title: Scientists told them, 'No, it's too dangerous,' but they did it anyway: Inside Japan's super-close asteroid flyby
summary: Japan's Hayabusa2 spacecraft conducted a daring super-close flyby of a near-Earth asteroid on July 5, capturing spectacular images despite initial warnings of danger. The mission required heated debate before teams approved the risky maneuver.

title: Another reusable rocket? Japan launches, lands RV-X  prototype (video)
summary: Japan successfully conducted the first flight test of its RV-X experimental reusable rocket prototype. The test included both launch and landing, marking a significant milestone for the country's reusable rocket technology.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 219 — SpaceX Goes to War!
summary: The latest episode of 'This Week In Space' podcast discusses recent space headlines, with a focus on SpaceX's activities. The episode covers key developments in the space industry over the past few weeks.

title: 'We knew we had a lot to live up to': As 'Aliens' turns 40, we chat to the legendary VFX masters who created the Alien Queen (interview)
summary: A 40th anniversary interview with VFX artists Shane Mahan and Lindsay MacGowan, who worked on the iconic Alien Queen in the 1986 film 'Aliens'. The discussion highlights their experiences and contributions to the movie's legendary visual effects.

title: Remembering 'Ulysses 31', the '80s cartoon that transported 'The Odyssey' to outer space
summary: The news reflects on 'Ulysses 31', an 80s cartoon that reimagined Homer's 'The Odyssey' in outer space, highlighting its iconic theme song and cultural impact.

title: These 'metallic' dunes on Mars look like sci-fi. What are they really?
summary: New images from ESA's Mars Express orbiter reveal unusual metallic-looking dunes on Mars. These formations are likely shaped by wind and mineral composition, creating a sci-fi-like appearance.

title: The Perseid meteor shower starts this week. Here's why you should look up now
summary: The Perseid meteor shower begins on July 17 alongside two others, making 'shooting stars' likely any night this month and next.

title: NASA Pushes New Wing Design to Find Structural Limits
summary: NASA tested a new lightweight, truss-braced wing design (SWEET-15) to find its structural limits and found promising results even beyond expected thresholds. The 15-foot experiment could pave the way for more efficient aircraft designs.

title: The Growing Crescent of Mars as NASA’s Psyche Mission Approaches
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured a composite image showing the crescent of Mars growing as the spacecraft approached for a gravity assist in 2026. The images were taken between May 2 and May 15, 2026.

title: Establishing a VTE Risk Score for Astronauts Algorithm
summary: NASA's Office of the Chief Health and Medical Officer (OCHMO) initiated a working group in April 2026 to review updated VTE case information and discuss research progress on mitigating venous thromboembolism risk during spaceflight.

title: NASA Awards Facilities Support Services Contract for Ames Research Center
summary: NASA has awarded a contract to Chugach Intelligence Solutions LLC for facilities support services at Ames Research Center, ensuring the center's operations and maintenance for five years.

title: NASA’s Chandra and IXPE Study Pulsar in Lighthouse Nebula
summary: NASA's Chandra and IXPE observatories have released a composite image studying a pulsar within the Lighthouse Nebula. The image combines X-ray data from both observatories to analyze the neutron star's magnetic field and spin.

title: La NASA invita a los medios de comunicación al lanzamiento del telescopio espacial Roman
summary: NASA has opened media accreditation for the launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, scheduled for no earlier than 7:20 a.m. EDT on August 30. The telescope's launch is part of NASA's ongoing space science missions.

title: NASA Invites Media to Roman Space Telescope, Crew-13 Launches
summary: NASA has opened media accreditation for the upcoming launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope and the SpaceX Crew-13 mission, both scheduled for the coming months. The Roman telescope is set to launch aboard a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket from Launch Complex 39A.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Images Details of Martian Surface During Flyby
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured enhanced-color images of Mars' surface during a flyby on May 15, 2026. The multispectral imager instrument collected data over six minutes as the spacecraft traveled from northeast to southwest.

title: NASA Welcomes Mauritius as 70th Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Mauritius has joined the Artemis Accords as the 70th signatory, becoming the seventh African country to commit to responsible space exploration. NASA's Deputy Administrator Matt Anderson welcomed the nation during a signing ceremony in Ébène.

title: A Tide-Fueled Trove of Biodiversity in Guinea-Bissau
summary: The Bijagós archipelago in Guinea-Bissau hosts diverse ecosystems like mudflats, beaches, and mangroves, supporting migratory shorebirds and sea turtles. This biodiversity is influenced by tidal patterns in the region.

title: Starship Flight 13: Booster 20 Aborts During Ignition
summary: Starship Flight 13 was aborted during ignition when Booster 20 failed to ignite properly. The launch attempt on July 16 at 5:45 pm CDT was halted due to the abort.

title: China’s first recovered booster returns to port as LandSpace aims for first land recovery
summary: China achieved a milestone with the first successful recovery of a booster, and LandSpace is now aiming for its first land recovery.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: Soyuz MS-29 successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome on July 14. This marks another mission supporting the ongoing operations and research aboard the ISS.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station (ISS), while SpaceX's Starship is preparing for its 13th test flight. Seven launches are scheduled worldwide this week.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key milestone ahead of its upcoming launch.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: Booster 20, a SpaceX rocket, completed a record-duration static fire test with all 33 engines. This marks a significant step toward SpaceX's next-generation launch capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays. The company reported significant gains in Electron launches during this period.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing significant progress with new launch pads being built for Stoke Space and Relativity Space. This marks another step in the region's revitalization.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved multiple milestones. The station aims to serve as a private orbital outpost.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The news source is a B2B website focused on the technology, electronics, and computing sectors, covering business-related content in these industries.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration.

title: Test firing at brand new green chemical propulsion lab
summary: A new green chemical propulsion lab conducted its first test firing. This marks a significant step in developing environmentally friendly propulsion technologies for space missions.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 ministerial meeting, detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across its programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm technology that could revolutionize space operations. It highlights the potential applications and advancements in robotic arm systems for space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Beyond Reach Labs Raises $10M Seed, Moves to NYC
summary: Beyond Reach Labs has secured a $10 million seed funding round and relocated its headquarters to New York City. The company aims to address challenges in spaceflight related to size, weight, and power constraints.

title: NASA Selects Starlink for Artemis III Mission
summary: NASA has awarded SpaceX a contract to provide laser communications for the Artemis III mission using Starlink technology. This marks a significant step in integrating commercial space communications into NASA's lunar exploration plans.

title: ISU Founder Shares a Vision Beyond Liquidation
summary: The ISU founder discussed the organization's future beyond liquidation with cofounder Bob Richards and Dean Emerita Angie Bukley. The conversation explored the aftermath of the liquidation and potential next steps.

title: SWISSto12 Closes $70M Series C
summary: SWISSto12, a European space company, has raised $70 million in a Series C funding round. The funds will support scaling operations to meet global demand for sovereign space hardware.

title: TraCSS Looms Large Over Mission Authorization Hearing
summary: A hearing reviewed the Office of Space Commerce's proposed framework for mission authorization, questioning its financial feasibility. The discussion highlighted concerns about the office's ability to manage the program.

title: Falcon 9 Sales Freeze Anxiety and Our Launch Ramp Forecast Model for New Rockets
summary: The news discusses concerns over a potential sales freeze of SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket, which could disrupt the space industry's reliance on reliable launch services. This uncertainty raises questions about the future of satellite businesses and the viability of new rockets.

title: Exclusive: Loft Orbital Scales Offering with Apex Bus Purchase
summary: Loft Orbital acquired new satellite bus inventory to support larger and more power-intensive customer payloads. The purchase aims to expand their offerings in the space industry.

title: NatSec Space Policy Nominees Face Questions from Senators
summary: Pentagon's space acquisition nominee testified that legacy military space programs are incompatible with modern acquisition reforms. Senators questioned nominees on space policy during a hearing.

title: Payload Field Guide: NSSL
summary: The U.S. Space Force expanded its National Security Space Launch (NSSL) program by adding two new launch providers on July 8, increasing the total number of contracted companies to seven.

title: Spinifex Space Launches With Australian Test Ranges
summary: Spinifex Space has launched operations offering end-to-end suborbital launch campaigns, private range access, and test and evaluation infrastructure. The company will utilize Australian test ranges for its services.

title: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace Partner for Lunar Exploration Ambitions
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to collaborate on lunar exploration. The partnership involves exchanging technical information and conducting system compatibility studies.

title: HawkEye 360 Details Tactical Direct Downlink in Valiant Shield Exercise
summary: HawkEye 360 demonstrated a tactical direct downlink during the Valiant Shield military exercise, showcasing a machine-to-machine pipeline with Lockheed Martin.

title: Starlink Laser Communications Will Support NASA Artemis III
summary: NASA's Artemis III mission will utilize SpaceX's Starlink mini laser terminals for downlinking imagery and video to mission control in Houston. The agreement was announced on Thursday.

title: BQP Secures SpaceWERX Funding for Quantum-Assisted AI for Space Domain Awareness
summary: BQP Federal LLC secured SpaceWERX funding to develop a quantum-assisted AI platform for space domain awareness. This marks the company's first federal contract.

title: Space Force Chief Nominee Schiess Counts Moving Target Indication Among Top Priorities
summary: U.S. Space Force Lt. Gen. Douglas Schiess prioritizes building space-based moving target indication systems and disrupting Chinese military space systems as key deterrence measures. This highlights the Space Force's focus on enhancing space domain awareness and counterspace capabilities.

title: Webinar Replay | Space-Based Data Centers: Inside the Race to Move Compute to Orbit
summary: A webinar discussed the emerging industry of space-based data centers, focusing on the business case, technical challenges, and commercial viability of moving computing infrastructure to orbit. Industry players debated the feasibility and potential of this innovative approach.

title: Keysight and Sateliot to Explore Blockchain Frameworks for 5G NTN Security
summary: Keysight Technologies and Sateliot will explore blockchain frameworks for 5G non-terrestrial network (NTN) security under an ESA-led program. The initiative aims to detect anomalies in satellite-based 5G communications.

title: Viasat Demonstrates Automotive Voice Call in BMW Platform
summary: Viasat demonstrated a satellite voice call integrated into a BMW Group vehicle's infotainment system during a 5G Automotive Association event. This showcases the integration of satellite communication in automotive technology.

title: AST SpaceMobile Discloses Delays to Launch Campaign, Explores Vertical Integration
summary: AST SpaceMobile has delayed its launch campaign to early 2027 and is exploring vertical integration. The company also announced a share sale to raise additional funds.

title: Swissto12 Secures $70M in Series C Round
summary: Swissto12 has raised $70 million in a Series C funding round to expand its manufacturing capabilities. The company reports strong revenue growth.
